title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Life Insurance With Accidental Death Benefit Rider
How do you prepare your loved ones to be financially stable for a tomorrow where you may not be to provide for them? You may start with savings. At times, savings may not grow the way you would like them to, which in turn may leave your loved ones in a financial crisis after you. Life insurance is one of the ways you might be able to ensure that in a future where you are no longer around, your family can take care of themselves.
What about an unfortunate event, such as an accident, that results in the demise of the provider of the family? The people who are left behind may still end up facing financial, possibly more so than usual, owing to the aftermath of the accident. Can life insurance help with this?
The answer is yes. If you add the accidental death benefit rider to your life insurance, you may get protection against the mortality of the person insured, occurring because of an accident. The accidental death benefit cover is usually available as an add-on rider and may be purchased with your life insurance policy, where the insurer offers the same.
Let’s take a look at what riders are, what an accidental death benefit rider is and how it works, as well as why you should consider taking an accidental death benefit add-on to your life insurance plan.
What are Riders in Life Insurance?
The primary benefit offered by life insurance plans is the death benefit. This is a benefit that can be claimed by the nominee or the beneficiary of the person insured if the insured were to meet their end. Another benefit offered by some of the life insurance plans may be the maturity benefit, which is the amount received by the person insured if they were to survive the policy term.
Although both these benefits may offer some significant cover for eventualities, they may not cover any special cases. Riders are the add-on features that may help you if you are looking to extend the coverage of your life insurance policy to any specific contingencies, such as critical illness, accidental death, or accidental permanent disability. These add-on features may also offer additional benefits such as a premium waiver, income benefit, and more.
Here are some of the commonly available life insurance riders:
Accidental Death Benefit Rider
Accidental Permanent Disability Rider
Critical Illness Rider
Family Income Benefit Rider
Waiver Of Premium Rider
Riders are usually available at a nominal cost, and the coverage duration may be the same as the duration of the life insurance plan.
What is an Accidental Death Benefit Rider?
Commonly, if the life assured were to pass away for any reason covered under the policy, the nominee, or the beneficiary can claim a death benefit. The amount they may receive as a death benefit is dependent on the sum assured of the plan. The sum assured is one of the key aspects of any policy and under many types of life insurance plans, the policyholder, while buying the policy, chooses it. This amount is meant to cover the liabilities left behind by the person who passed away, as well as cover the needs of the family in their absence. However, the aftermath of an accidental death may be significant. Thus, life insurance with an accidental death benefit rider may offer an amount in addition to the sum assured if the person insured were to pass away due to an accident.
Why Should You Opt for Accidental Death Benefit Rider?
Unfortunate mishaps are not something one can anticipate. They may happen to anyone in the blink of an eye. While you may do your best to avoid them, and escape unscathed, they may have the potential to cause some irreparable damage. For some, it may even result in fatality. This may be immediate or after prolonged suffering.
In either case, the family of the person affected may end up going through unfathomable emotional turmoil. In addition to this, if the person passes away, they may also end up facing medical bills while having to deal with the loss of a loved one. This loss may end up being even more pronounced if the person they lost was a provider of the family.
Having life insurance with an accidental death benefit rider may help them deal with the costs they may have to face after your passing. This may help them shift the focus on their emotional recovery and get their respective lives back on track.
An accidental death benefit rider might be a way to boost your life insurance policy. It may help support your family posthumously by preparing for it beforehand. The additional sum they may receive over and above the sum assured might help them cope with any unforeseen expenses they may have to face after an accident.
When buying life insurance with an accidental death benefit rider, one should read carefully through the inclusions and exclusions of the policy. The details of these may vary across policies as well as insurance providers.
Buying an accidental death benefit rider might be a way to fortify your life insurance policy against more dire possibilities. If you buy life insurance with an accidental death benefit rider, you may not necessarily have to use it. However, since the future remains unseen and uncertain, it may be ideal to have such an add-on cover.
Buying an accidental death benefit rider comes with a nominal additional premium. If you are keen on this add-on, use a life insurance calculator to see how the addition of this rider can affect your premium amount. Furthermore, check whether the policy you want to buy offers the riders you seek, such as the accidental death benefit one.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
Life Insurance With Accidental Death Benefit Rider
How do you prepare your loved ones to be financially stable for a tomorrow where you may not be there to provide for them? You may start with savings. At times, savings may not grow the way you would like them to, which in turn may leave your loved ones in a financial crisis after you. Life insurance is one of the ways you might be able to ensure that in a future where you are no longer around, your family can take care of themselves financially.
What about an unfortunate event, such as an accident, that results in the demise of the provider of the family? The people who are left behind may still end up facing financial distress, possibly more so than usual, owing to the aftermath of the accident. Can life insurance help with this?
The answer is yes. Life insurance covers death due to accident as well. If you opt to add the accidental death benefit rider to your life insurance, on payment of additional nominal premium, you may get additional protection against the mortality of the person insured, occurring because of an accident, subject to the satisfaction of the terms and conditions of the rider. The accidental death benefit cover is usually available as an add-on rider and may be purchased with your life insurance policy, where the insurer offers the same.
Let’s take a look at what riders are, what an accidental death benefit rider is and how it works, as well as why you may consider taking an accidental death benefit add-on to your life insurance plan.
What are Riders in Life Insurance?
The primary benefit offered by life insurance plans is the death benefit. This is a benefit that can be claimed by the nominee of the person insured if the insured dies. Another benefit offered by some of the life insurance plans may be the maturity benefit, which is the amount received by the person insured if they were to survive the policy term.
Although both these benefits may offer some significant cover for eventualities, they may or may not cover any special cases. Riders are the add-on features that may help you if you are looking to extend the coverage of your life insurance policy to any specific contingencies, such as critical illness, accidental death, or accidental permanent disability. These add-on features may also offer additional benefits such as a premium waiver, income benefit, and more.
Here are some of the commonly available life insurance riders, among others:
 Accidental Death Benefit Rider  Accidental Permanent Disability Rider  Critical Illness Rider  Waiver Of Premium Rider
Riders are usually available at a nominal cost, and the coverage duration may be the same as the duration of the life insurance plan, provided all due premiums are paid.
What is an Accidental Death Benefit Rider?
Commonly, if the life assured were to pass away for any reason covered under the policy, the nominee, can claim a death benefit. The amount they may receive as a death benefit is dependent on the sum assured of the plan. The sum assured is one of the key aspects of any policy and under many types of life insurance plans, the policyholder, while buying the policy, chooses it. This amount is meant to cover the liabilities left behind by the person who passed away, as well as cover the needs of the family in their absence. However, the aftermath of an accidental death may be significant. Thus, life insurance with an accidental death benefit rider may offer an amount in addition to the sum assured if the person insured were to pass away due to an accident depending on the terms and conditions of the rider.
Why Should You Opt for Accidental Death Benefit Rider?
Unfortunate mishaps are not something one can anticipate. They may happen to anyone in the blink of an eye. While you may do your best to avoid them, and escape unscathed, they may have the potential to cause some irreparable damage. For some, it may even result in fatality. This may be immediate or after prolonged suffering.
In either case, the family of the person affected may end up going through emotional turmoil. In addition to this, if the person passes away, they may also end up facing medical bills while having to deal with the loss of a loved one. This loss may end up being even more pronounced if the person they lost was a provider of the family.
Having life insurance with an accidental death benefit rider may help them deal with the costs they may have to face after life assured passes away.
An accidental death benefit rider might be a way to boost your life insurance policy. It may help support your family posthumously by preparing for it beforehand. The additional sum they may receive over and above the policy sum assured might help them cope with any unforeseen expenses they may have to face after an accident.
When buying life insurance with an accidental death benefit rider, one should read carefully through the inclusions and exclusions of the policy as well as rider. The details of these may vary across policies as well as insurance providers.
Buying an accidental death benefit rider might be a way to fortify your life goals against more dire possibilities.
Buying an accidental death benefit rider comes with a nominal additional premium. If you are keen on this add-on, use a life insurance calculator to see how the addition of this rider can affect your premium amount. Furthermore, check whether the policy you want to buy offers the riders you seek, such as the accidental death benefit one.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
